rationale: The citing paper discusses Fisher, Gallino, and Xu (2019) in two places as a concrete empirical study showing that faster delivery improves online and offline sales. It uses that evidence to support the broader claim that service reliability has important demand consequences, then contrasts prior demand-side work with its own focus on competitive service-reliability decisions by firms. This is more than a passing list citation because the paper explicitly summarizes the cited study's design and findings to motivate its research gap.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior empirical foundation that service reliability improvements can create demand benefits. It then builds its positioning on that base by shifting attention from demand effects to firms' competitive reliability choices in airlines.
